name: variable_width_shift_in_always_ff
description: Code should include a right shift operation (>>) where the shift amount is a multi-bit register variable rather than a constant. This creates variable-length shift behavior that can cause unpredictable synthesis results.
snippet: if (b >> e) begin d = e; end

name: self_canceling_arithmetic_condition
description: Code should include an arithmetic expression that subtracts a variable from itself (e.g., c - c), which always evaluates to zero. This creates a constant-false condition that can confuse synthesis optimizations.

name: combinational_toggle_feedback
description: Code should include a register assignment that toggles its value (!e) inside a combinational always block. This creates an unstable feedback loop where the register changes every evaluation cycle, potentially causing synthesis tools to fail to converge.

name: dff_inverter_merge_oscillation
description: Code should include a latch inference pattern with feedback that causes synthesis tools to oscillate between inverted and non-inverted latch states. The pattern involves registers with toggle behavior driving latch control signals, leading to infinite loops in DFF optimization passes.
snippet: always @(*) begin if (b >> e) begin d = e; end e = !e; end
